title: Exogenous event like 9/11 — early COVID macro thesis
html:
TL;DR

  • Five-line macro thesis written March 24, 2020 — 11 days into the COVID crash. Frames the pandemic as an exogenous event like 9/11 layered onto an already-stretched economy ("ropes conditions today, like the dot com bubble").
  • Predicts: government injects money → works temporarily → bigger downturn → only recovers when life "returns to normal" → recovery is gradual.
  • Yahir is 17, calling the macro environment in real time. The "shit is going to get worse before it gets better" line aged well over the next 18 months.
  • Specimen of value investing-style top-down thinking at adolescent age. Predates his Amazon FBA hustle and short-market notes (2020 04 23 go to diamond).
  • Connects to covid pandemic era cluster.

Key quotes

"EXOGENOUS EVENT. - like 9/11, already underlined with ropes conditions today, like the dot com bubble"

"Last ditch effort by govern,ent to inject money into economy / Works temporarily, but when it doesn't, it leads to major downturn"

"Will only get better when life 'returns to normal', meaning people go about like regular without lockdowns, but that in itself will be gradual"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Earliest macro-thesis specimen in the corpus. Companion to 2020 04 23 go to diamond (his April watch-list with "1987 crash" and "short market" notes).
  • Establishes the pattern: Yahir frames new events through historical analogy (9/11, dot-com, 1987). Same move at scale in 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide (frames AI search as the new SEO).
  • Confirms covid pandemic era as the formative episode of his investing brain.

Open questions

  • What securities was he watching at this exact moment? (TDA custodial account opened around this time per 2020 04 23 go to diamond).
  • Did this thesis hold up in his head through the recovery, or did he flip to "stocks are rigged" later?

Wikilinks
